title: Weekly Market Update: Tech Stocks Lead Market Lower, Basic Materials Gain
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Tech stocks led declines, while basic materials gained in the latest market update. Key tech companies fell after earnings, but basic materials stocks rose. For
excerpt: Tech stocks led declines, while basic materials gained in the latest market update. Key tech companies fell after earnings, but basic materials stocks rose. For detailed information, visit Morningstar's weekly market update.

content.introduction: Tech stocks led declines, while basic materials gained in the latest market update. Key tech companies fell after earnings, but basic materials stocks rose. For detailed information, visit Morningstar's weekly market update.
body: [](https://www.morningstar.com/login?destination=https://www.morningstar.com/markets/weekly-market-update-stock-gainers-losers). *   [Stocks](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks). *   [Stocks](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks). *   [All Products and Services](https://www.morningstar.com/business). 2.   [Markets](https://www.morningstar.com/markets). [Vertiv Holdings Co Class A (VRT)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/vrt/quote). [Bloom Energy Corp Class A (BE)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/be/quote). [Oracle Corp (ORCL)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/orcl/quote). [EchoStar Corp Class A (SATS)](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/sats/quote). Technology led the declines, falling 2.18% for the week as AI heavyweights Oracle [ORCL](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnys/orcl/quote) and Broadcom [AVGO](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/xnas/avgo/quote) fell sharply after earnings. [View All](https://www.morningstar.com/markets). [More from Author](https://www.morningstar.com/people/frank-lee). *   #### [34 New 4-Star Stocks This Week Frank Lee Feb 9, 2026](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/34-new-4-star-stocks-this-week). *   #### [25 New 5-Star Stocks This Week Frank Lee Feb 9, 2026](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/25-new-5-star-stocks-this-week). *   #### [Weekly Market Update: Stocks Remain Flat as Consumer Defensives Rise and Financial Services Fall Frank Lee Jan 16, 2026](https://www.morningstar.com/markets/weekly-market-update-stocks-remain-flat-consumer-defensives-rise-financial-services-fall). *   #### [Weekly Markets Wrap: Stocks Slipped as 2025 Turned to 2026 Frank Lee Jan 2, 2026](https://www.morningstar.com/markets/weekly-markets-wrap-stocks-slipped-2025-turned-2026). *   #### [Weekly Market Wrap: Stocks Extend 2025 Rally as Basic Materials, Tech Rise Frank Lee Dec 26, 2025](https://www.morningstar.com/stocks/weekly-market-wrap-stocks-extend-2025-rally-basic-materials-tech-rise). ![Image 15](https://www.morningstar.com/assets/img/editorial-content.717b26b.svg). *   [All Products & Services](https://www.morningstar.com/business). *   [About Us](https://www.morningstar.com/company/about-us). *   [Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information](https://www.morningstar.com/company/privacy-policy/do-not-sell-or-share).
Technology stocks remain a major driver of market growth, supported by innovation, productivity, and Artificial Intelligence (AI) investment.

The dive in chips yesterday keyed off a Wall Street Journal report about **OpenAI** missing internal demand targets that appeared to be "a perfectly timed piece to spoil the party in chips," said Nathan Peterson, director of derivatives research and strategy at the Schwab Center for Financial Research (SCFR). 1. **Magnificent Seven AI spending in focus: Alphabet** (GOOGL), **Amazon** (AMZN), **Meta Platforms** (META), and **Microsoft** (MSFT) all come out with earnings after the close today, meaning market participants might want to consider tracking where their stocks head in overnight trading for clues into Thursday's action on Wall Street. "Investors still appear to be relatively forgiving toward rampant AI infrastructure spending, but the days of treating Mag 7 as a bullish monolithic trade appear to be over," my colleague Peterson said in Schwab's [tech earnings preview](/learn/story/tech-earnings-preview). * **Earnings yesterday** generally continued the trend of major **S&P 500** companies beating analysts' expectations, with many executives reporting resilient consumer demand.
